outlook.headline: Memory prices are still rising, and the pace is accelerating
outlook.summary: The read-through is decisively positive: memory price increases are showing up across Micron, distributors like Avnet and Arrow, and adjacent beneficiaries citing stronger pricing/mix and margin lift. The mention trend is also accelerating sharply, which suggests this is not a one-off but a broadening pricing upcycle into 2026Q1; watch whether higher prices start to bite mobile/consumer demand or simply pull orders forward.

drivers.label: Broad price increases in memory channels
drivers.detail: Avnet and Arrow both said memory saw the biggest price increases, with Arrow also seeing customers order ahead to lock supply.

drivers.label: Sustained seller pricing power
drivers.detail: Micron said higher pricing is lifting gross margins, while TSMC described solid pricing trend and warned only against dramatic changes.

drivers.label: Downstream pricing/mix uplift
drivers.detail: AMD, Broadcom, and Analog Devices all tied stronger pricing actions to better mix, ASPs, or margin support, reinforcing that higher memory pricing is feeding through the chain.

drivers.label: Expectation of further increases
drivers.detail: Texas Instruments said prices may go up in 2H, and Amkor expects pricing to increase through the year, indicating the upcycle is still in progress.

drivers.label: Tight supply supports pricing
drivers.detail: GlobalFoundries pointed to tight supply and capacity constraints as enabling price increases and customer prepayments, a constructive backdrop for pricing discipline.

risks: Mobile/consumer demand could weaken if higher pricing starts to constrain volumes, as hinted by Teradyne's weaker pricing and availability commentary.
If customers finish pulling in orders to lock supply, near-term pricing momentum could cool.
Some mentions were neutral or non-committal, so the breadth of the uptrend still needs confirmation in the next quarter.
